jokes.text: Now the story of a wealthy family who lost everything and the one son who had no choice but to keep them all together.
jokes.setup: Narrator presents Michael as having 'no choice' but to keep family together
jokes.punchline: The ironic framing suggests he actually does have choices but is compelled by circumstance/guilt
jokes.explanation: Classic opening hook that's both exposition and comedy - the 'no choice' framing is darkly funny

jokes.text: Who the hell to? / Who do you think? Rita. / Her? / Why?
jokes.setup: Michael announces engagement to Rita
jokes.punchline: Parents' immediate dismissive reactions - 'Her?' and 'Why?' show complete lack of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work - Lucille's immediate judgment is exactly what we expect

jokes.text: Well, first of all, she needs a green card to stay in the country. / There it is. / She needs a green card to stay in the country, because, secondly...
jokes.setup: Michael starts with the practical reason (green card)
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s 'There it is' assumes that's the ONLY reason, forcing Michael to backtrack
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s cynical assumption interrupts Michael's attempt at romantic justification

jokes.text: She was special. / Are houses terribly hard to make? / Actually, the hardest part is finding the land. / But she wasn't smart. / Instead of making houses, maybe you should make land.
jokes.setup: Narrator says 'She was special' followed by her innocent question about houses
jokes.punchline: Rita's suggestion to 'make land' reveals her complete misunderstanding of basic concepts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - 'special' sounds positive until we hear her logic. The narrator's correction is perfectly timed

jokes.text: For Rita had only mentally progressed to the level of a first grader. / There's no land on the ocean. / You're brilliant.
jokes.setup: Narrator reveals Rita's mental capacity, Rita makes obvious statement
jokes.punchline: Michael calls her 'brilliant' for stating something elementary
jokes.explanation: The dramatic irony is painful and hilarious - we know what Michael doesn't

jokes.text: All right, now look, just because a woman gets pregnant doesn't mean you have to marry her. Too many lives have been ruined because some cheap waitress at a HoJo said she used an IUD. / It was Stuckey's. / But I believed you.
jokes.setup: George Sr. gives marriage advice about pregnant women and waitresses
jokes.punchline: Lucille corrects the restaurant chain and confirms she lied about birth control
jokes.explanation: Dark family history revealed casually. Lucille's correction of the restaurant detail is perfectly in character

jokes.text: This is funny! / He's gay! / Where's your hand? / Oh. I'm having my high school ring put on it, and my hook is stuck in the staircar.
jokes.setup: Parents comment on Michael's relationship, Buster is asked about his hand
jokes.punchline: Buster's hand is stuck because he's getting a high school ring put on his hook
jokes.explanation: Absurd physical situation perfectly fits Buster's character - adult getting high school ring on prosthetic hook

jokes.text: Domo arigoto, Mister Roboto...
jokes.setup: Buster is stuck in some mechanical situation
jokes.punchline: He quotes the robot-themed song while literally trapped by machinery
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits the situation - Buster as trapped robot

jokes.text: And I like to keep the light on. / That way I can see if you have a monster. / Night-night. / Can you shut the door on your way out?
jokes.setup: Michael explains why he keeps lights on, Rita gives childish reason
jokes.punchline: Rita's 'monster' comment and dismissal reveals how young her mindset is
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - the audience knows what Michael doesn't about Rita's mental state

jokes.text: Let's just all relax, and remind ourselves that we don't have any money. / She's already gotten our money!
jokes.setup: Michael tries to calm fears about money-grabbing
jokes.punchline: Lucille immediately contradicts him - Rita already has their money
jokes.explanation: Lucille's paranoia immediately undermines Michael's logical point

jokes.text: Oh, big deal, I had that same basic idea years ago.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Rita's ocean town idea
jokes.punchline: George Sr. claims he had the same ridiculous idea first
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s ego makes him claim credit for an obviously bad idea

jokes.text: Ah, here comes the bride. / Hey. What are you doing in a wheelchair? / No, no, no, it's a dolly to help videotape your nuptials.
jokes.setup: Tobias announces bride's arrival, Michael sees him in wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Tobias claims it's camera equipment while clearly having mobility issues
jokes.explanation: Tobias's denial of his obvious health problems for a flimsy excuse

jokes.text: Okay, so my legs and my left arm occasionally 'go to sleep,' and you want to call it a health problem.
jokes.setup: Tobias is clearly having serious mobility issues
jokes.punchline: He minimizes partial paralysis as limbs 'going to sleep'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias denial - catastrophic understatement of serious symptoms. The air quotes around 'go to sleep' make it even funnier

jokes.text: No, thank you, I don't need your help. / It does seem like you've been having a whole lot of trouble since the hair transplants.
jokes.setup: Tobias struggles to get up, refuses help
jokes.punchline: Michael connects his problems to hair transplants
jokes.explanation: Reveals Tobias's vanity procedures are causing serious health issues

jokes.text: Maybe you should see a bubuman. / A... 'bubuman'? / Oh, uh, a doctor. It's a British expression. Like they say 'go-up box' instead of 'elevator.'
jokes.setup: Rita suggests Tobias see a 'bubuman'
jokes.punchline: She claims these are British terms, making up fake expressions
jokes.explanation: Rita's childlike invention of fake British words is both innocent and absurd

jokes.text: Oh, like when they say 'poofter' to mean 'tourist,' yes. / Boy, that Rita's a smarty.
jokes.setup: Tobias tries to join in with British slang examples
jokes.punchline: He uses an actual British slur thinking it means tourist, calling Rita smart
jokes.explanation: Layered joke - Tobias doesn't know what 'poofter' means (ironic given his sexuality), calls Rita smart for made-up words

jokes.text: Maybe they could walk. / Across the ocean? / If it's not too deep.
jokes.setup: Maeby asks how to get characters from LA to Japan
jokes.punchline: Rita suggests walking across the ocean if it's not too deep
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rita logic - treating ocean depth like a puddle. Sets up the episode title joke

jokes.text: No, deep is good. People are going to say, 'What the hell just happened? I better say I like it.' 'Cause nobody wants to seem stupid.
jokes.setup: Rita's nonsensical movie suggestion
jokes.punchline: Maeby explains how confusing art gets praised because people fear seeming stupid
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on pretentious art and audience psychology, fits Maeby's cynical Hollywood perspective

jokes.text: The Ocean Walker. / Holy crap, that's going to look good on a hat.
jokes.setup: Rita names the nonsensical movie concept
jokes.punchline: Maeby immediately thinks about merchandising the ridiculous title
jokes.explanation: Sets up episode title while showing Hollywood's focus on marketing over story quality

jokes.text: You want me to do a magic show at the wedding. I'm on it. / So glad we pushed through that. / No magic.
jokes.setup: Michael asks for a favor, Gob assumes it's magic without listening
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately shuts down the magic idea Gob created
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - assumes everything involves magic, doesn't listen, creates his own disappointment

jokes.text: Put her out! Put her out! / Tracy! / Thank God we already did the pictures.
jokes.setup: Reference to Gob's previous magic disaster
jokes.punchline: Gob's relief that photos were taken before the bride caught fire
jokes.explanation: Callback to Gob's history of wedding disasters, his priorities are hilariously wrong

jokes.text: I am going to get you the single healthiest call girl that this town has ever seen.
jokes.setup: Gob commits to being the best witness possible
jokes.punchline: He interprets 'witness' as procuring a prostitute
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob misunderstanding - takes the role seriously but completely misinterprets it. 'Healthiest' is a nice touch

jokes.text: Oh, I'm not going to spend this kind of money and not watch.
jokes.setup: Michael clarifies Gob just needs to watch
jokes.punchline: Gob confirms he'll watch the prostitute he's planning to hire
jokes.explanation: Doubles down on the misunderstanding in a creepy way that's perfectly Gob

jokes.text: Hey, hello. Well, look at you two, Tweedle Dee, Tweedle Dum. / Michael didn't mean it in that way. / How could he, he didn't even know.
jokes.setup: Michael greets Rita and Lindsay with nursery rhyme reference
jokes.punchline: Narrator clarifies he doesn't know he's accurately describing Rita's mental state
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Michael accidentally uses perfect metaphor without realizing it

jokes.text: You guys have these jackets on inside out? / Yeah, that way you see the label. / Yeah, I mean, that's what you're paying for, right? It's a great statement on fashion. / Plus, it doesn't tickle your neck.
jokes.setup: Michael notices inside-out jackets
jokes.punchline: Rita's innocent reason (neck tickling) undercuts Lindsay's pretentious fashion explanation
jokes.explanation: Rita's childlike practicality versus Lindsay's fashion pretension

jokes.text: King... takes his queen... and showers her with diamonds! / Clubs. / Club sauce. / He covers her with club sauce.
jokes.setup: Gob performs magic trick with playing cards and liquid
jokes.punchline: Rita identifies clubs as 'club sauce' when the trick goes wrong
jokes.explanation: Rita's innocent mislabeling of what's probably alcohol or lighter fluid

jokes.text: I don't appreciate the dry British humor.
jokes.setup: Rita makes innocent comments about magic
jokes.punchline: Gob interprets her literal observations as sophisticated sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Gob misreads Rita's innocent comments as witty putdowns due to her accent

jokes.text: Oh, lighter fluid--like Uncle Trevor uses! / But wherever did it come from?
jokes.setup: Magic trick involves lighter fluid appearing
jokes.punchline: Rita casually mentions uncle's use of lighter fluid while wondering about the 'magic'
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about Uncle Trevor while Rita remains innocent about the trick

jokes.text: How long can you hold your breath? / Oh, long. One time, 20 minutes. / Actually, her record was 20 seconds.
jokes.setup: Gob asks about breath-holding for a trick
jokes.punchline: Rita claims 20 minutes, narrator immediately corrects to 20 seconds
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Rita's delusion versus reality, with immediate narrator correction

jokes.text: Now, I just need to find a trick that puts you out of oxygen for 20 minutes.
jokes.setup: Rita claims she can hold breath for 20 minutes
jokes.punchline: Gob plans a potentially lethal trick based on her false claim
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of Gob planning to accidentally kill Rita based on her misinformation

jokes.text: What if I find a trick that has her float to the alter or something? / No, no. Love for her to walk. And not to drown. / Or fly through the air. Or catch on fire.
jokes.setup: Gob suggests wedding magic tricks
jokes.punchline: Michael lists all the ways Gob's tricks could kill Rita
jokes.explanation: References Gob's history of dangerous magic accidents, builds to absurd safety requirements

jokes.text: Is she allergic to cat fur? / Oop-- no tigers.
jokes.setup: Gob asks about allergies
jokes.punchline: Michael preemptively bans tigers, suggesting Gob was considering them
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from cat fur to tigers, Michael knows Gob too well

jokes.text: Well, I-I-I was hoping maybe for more than just our rooms to touch. / I was... thinking maybe we could be... in the same room. / Be in the same bed. / Oh, I see what you mean, a real sleepover.
jokes.setup: Michael awkwardly tries to suggest intimacy
jokes.punchline: Rita interprets his sexual advance as a children's sleepover
jokes.explanation: Perfectly painful cringe comedy - the audience knows what Michael is asking for but Rita doesn't

jokes.text: And my tiny teddy. / Good. Yes, get that.
jokes.setup: Rita mentions getting her 'tiny teddy'
jokes.punchline: Michael enthusiastically agrees thinking it's lingerie, not knowing it's a stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Another painful misunderstanding - 'tiny teddy' sounds sexual to Michael but isn't

jokes.text: Hot potato, hot potato... / Hot po-ta-to... Hot 'potat-o'...
jokes.setup: George Michael offers to play young people's music
jokes.punchline: Rita sings children's song while George Michael tries to make it cool
jokes.explanation: George Michael's realization something is wrong as Rita sings kids' songs

jokes.text: That's when he first grew concerned.
jokes.setup: George Michael witnesses Rita singing children's songs
jokes.punchline: Narrator's understated description of his realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated narration of George Michael's dawning awareness

jokes.text: I know what it is, Trevor, I've seen your secret magazine. / Spoon and figgy pudding. / Rabbit downdum druggary's hollow. / And I want to have pop-pop.
jokes.setup: Rita claims to know about sex from Trevor's magazine
jokes.punchline: She recites nonsense words she thinks describe sex acts
jokes.explanation: Rita's innocent gibberish about sex is both funny and deeply uncomfortable

jokes.text: Well, the very fact that you call it that, tells me you're not ready. / Oh, and for your information, that's a cricket magazine.
jokes.setup: Rita calls sex 'pop-pop'
jokes.punchline: Trevor's line echoes earlier quote, reveals magazine is actually about cricket
jokes.explanation: Callback to Arrested Development's running 'pop-pop' joke while clarifying the innocent magazine

jokes.text: I mean, it's not your fault your parents were cousins, but... here we are.
jokes.setup: Trevor explains Rita's limitations
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals Rita is product of incest with 'here we are' understatement
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic explanation delivered with British understatement

jokes.text: Well, Michael will be my cousin soon enough, cause we're getting married. / Married?! / Married! / Totally out of the question! / Married! Married! Married!
jokes.setup: Rita mentions marriage
jokes.punchline: Rita repeats 'married' like a mantra while Trevor protests
jokes.explanation: Rita's childlike repetition versus Trevor's adult panic

jokes.text: I'm putting the invisible locks on the door. / That's not fair. / Lock. Lock. Lock. / That's not fair! / It's just not fair! / Lock. Lock.
jokes.setup: Trevor threatens to lock Rita in
jokes.punchline: He pretends to apply invisible locks while Rita believes it
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable scene of adult manipulating mentally disabled person with pretend locks

jokes.text: Michael briefly considered faking the voice. / I... She's in the shower. / I'll wait. / Let's wait.
jokes.setup: Michael claims Rita is in shower to avoid putting her on phone
jokes.punchline: George Sr. insists on waiting, forcing Michael to maintain the lie
jokes.explanation: Michael trapped by his own lie as George Sr. won't let it go

jokes.text: Wow, that Blossom blew up, huh?
jokes.setup: George Sr. making small talk while waiting
jokes.punchline: Comments on dated TV show while under house arrest
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s dated pop culture reference while trapped watching old TV

jokes.text: And that local calls were $2.95 a minute. / Come on! / That's where they get you.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes Rita isn't coming
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the expensive hotel phone charges, Michael's outrage
jokes.explanation: Adds insult to injury - romantic disappointment plus expensive phone charges

jokes.text: You can't unlock this door? / I can't even see half the locks! / They're invisible. / Trevor's the only one who can see them. / I'm trapped!
jokes.setup: Michael tries to get Rita to open her door
jokes.punchline: Rita explains she can't see the invisible locks that don't exist
jokes.explanation: Rita's complete belief in the invisible locks shows her mental state

jokes.text: And so, Michael set about rescuing the beautiful, sweet, mentally challenged woman he'd fallen in love with, without realizing she was mentally challenged.
jokes.setup: Michael decides to rescue Rita
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact description of the dramatic irony
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator summary that highlights the dramatic irony at the heart of the episode

jokes.text: I hate that ( bleep ) doll.
jokes.setup: Michael and Rita escape via window
jokes.punchline: Trevor's angry outburst gets bleeped out
jokes.explanation: Trevor's frustration boiling over into profanity about Rita

jokes.text: Let the hand-outs begin! / There's not gonna be any hand-outs, and she is not gonna marry your son.
jokes.setup: George Sr. assumes Trevor wants money
jokes.punchline: Trevor immediately corrects him - he's trying to prevent the marriage
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s assumptions about people's motivations prove wrong

jokes.text: You are not gonna get Rita's millions just because she's mentally challenged. / Millions? / Mentally challenged? / Shh! It's not polite.
jokes.setup: Trevor reveals Rita has millions and is mentally challenged
jokes.punchline: Lucille's only concern is the improper language, not the revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - focuses on propriety while missing the bigger implications

jokes.text: Yes, it's funny how Americans don't pick up on that. I think it's 'cause of her accent and the plastic surgery she's been able to afford.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how people miss Rita's condition
jokes.punchline: Trevor explains Americans can't detect mental disability due to accent and wealth
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on how class and accent can mask obvious issues

jokes.text: We think he may be mentally challenged. / What?
jokes.setup: George Sr. doesn't understand something
jokes.punchline: They suggest he's mentally challenged too, shocking Trevor
jokes.explanation: Bluths turn the tables on Trevor with their own diagnosis

jokes.text: We have got 24 hours to get them married before Michael figures out what she really is. / This the first time in two years you've sounded like George Bluth.
jokes.setup: Lucille outlines their scheming plan
jokes.punchline: George Sr. notes this is the most devious she's sounded recently
jokes.explanation: George Sr. appreciates Lucille returning to her scheming form

jokes.text: Mere fact that you call it that tells me that you are ready.
jokes.setup: Rita says she wants 'sexual relations'
jokes.punchline: Michael reverses his earlier quote, not knowing Rita learned the phrase from Trevor
jokes.explanation: Callback that works ironically - Michael thinks Rita using adult language shows maturity

jokes.text: I was videotaping Rita. / Let's just say it would be cute if it was someone much younger.
jokes.setup: George Michael awkwardly tells his father something difficult
jokes.punchline: He reveals Rita's behavior would only be appropriate for a child
jokes.explanation: George Michael's delicate way of revealing Rita's mental state

jokes.text: It's the plastic fruit, Dad. / It's very convincing fruit, okay? / Is she going back in for another? / Well, you know, they don't even have bananas in England, okay, son?
jokes.setup: George Michael explains Rita was eating plastic fruit
jokes.punchline: Michael desperately tries to rationalize it with false facts about England
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate denial in the face of obvious evidence, making up facts about England

jokes.text: I think she might be... retar... retarded. / I'm sorry. / I'm really sorry.
jokes.setup: George Michael struggles to say the difficult word
jokes.punchline: His repeated apologies for using the term
jokes.explanation: George Michael's discomfort with the clinical term shows his good character

jokes.text: Say hello to my tiny teddy. / Oh! Tiny Teddy!
jokes.setup: Rita produces her 'tiny teddy'
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes it's literally a tiny teddy bear, not lingerie
jokes.explanation: The payoff to the earlier setup - Michael's horror at realizing his mistake

jokes.text: Oh, you teach kids, huh? / Oh, I like to think they teach me. / That way I can see if you have a monster. / Be careful.
jokes.setup: Michael's dawning realization through flashback
jokes.punchline: All of Rita's previous statements now make horrifying sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant recontextualization of earlier scenes - everything takes on new meaning

jokes.text: I've made a huge mistake.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes Rita's mental state
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase for the massive realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of Michael's catchphrase for this major realization

jokes.text: For instance, you've never told me how many houses there are in the British Parliament. / Seven. / Are there? / The answer is three. / Does sound about right. / Three is the correct answer.
jokes.setup: Michael tests Rita with a fake civics question
jokes.punchline: Rita confidently gives wrong answers to Michael's made-up question
jokes.explanation: Michael's test is nonsensical but Rita's confident wrong answers confirm his fears

jokes.text: Rita corny, Michael. / You know, I think that we should wait until after we're married. / Married! / Married, married, married!
jokes.setup: Rita uses elementary language, Michael deflects intimacy
jokes.punchline: Rita's excited repetition of 'married' like a child
jokes.explanation: Rita's childlike excitement contrasts with Michael's new discomfort

jokes.text: And Michael felt he deserved a $15 thing of candy beans.
jokes.setup: Michael deals with the stress of his realization
jokes.punchline: Narrator notes his expensive hotel candy indulgence
jokes.explanation: Small joke about expensive hotel snacks and Michael's justified stress eating

jokes.text: Show them the waffle bar, Larry. / It's over there. / And check out banner, Michael!
jokes.setup: Surprise wedding setup being shown off
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous banner mistakes in the show
jokes.explanation: Callback to the show's running gag about misspelled banners

jokes.text: I want to get married and have sexual relations like you promised. / Where did you get this firecracker? / I... love her, Michael.
jokes.setup: Rita announces her expectations loudly
jokes.punchline: George Sr. admires what he thinks is Rita's boldness, Michael's horror
jokes.explanation: George Sr. misreads Rita's innocent announcement as sophisticated, Michael's growing horror

jokes.text: Yeah. With the exception of the retard thing.
jokes.setup: Lucille repeats Michael's romantic speech about little surprises
jokes.punchline: She casually mentions Rita's disability as a minor exception
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Lucille - matter-of-fact cruelty delivered as if it's a minor detail

jokes.text: I'm crazy about the little dullard.
jokes.setup: Michael confronts Lucille about knowing Rita's condition
jokes.punchline: Lucille uses affectionate tone while using a cruel descriptor
jokes.explanation: Lucille's term of endearment that's simultaneously cruel - perfect character consistency

jokes.text: Larry go to a mirror! / You're fired. / You're fired. / What?! / It's over.
jokes.setup: Michael fires someone in anger
jokes.punchline: Lucille also fires someone, creating confusion about who can fire whom
jokes.explanation: Competing authority figures firing people in their anger

jokes.text: For instance, in some states, it's legal to marry your own cousin. / California's blocked it twice, but that's only because they tacked it onto an estate law thing that wasn't gonna pass. / We had the signatures...
jokes.setup: George Michael tries to help with marriage advice
jokes.punchline: He reveals disturbing knowledge about cousin marriage laws and political campaigns
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation about George Michael's research into cousin marriage, perfectly in character

jokes.text: She probably can't list the seven Houses of Parliament. / There's three.
jokes.setup: Michael creates another fake civics test
jokes.punchline: George Michael corrects Michael's made-up number
jokes.explanation: Callback to Michael's earlier made-up test, George Michael playing along

jokes.text: That's not gonna be easy. She's an Olympic Silver. / Just figured that one out.
jokes.setup: Need to catch Rita who's running away
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes Rita's Olympic achievement was in Special Olympics
jokes.explanation: Perfect recontextualization - Rita's Olympic status now makes horrifying sense

jokes.text: Okay. Well, I'll have to go stop production on The Ocean Walker.
jokes.setup: Michael's relationship with Rita is ending
jokes.punchline: Maeby matter-of-factly mentions stopping the ridiculous movie
jokes.explanation: Callback to the episode title joke - the absurd movie is being cancelled

jokes.text: Maybe you're not smart, either. / I didn't know until they told me. / No, I'm just a narcissist who was too self-involved to see the truth.
jokes.setup: Rita suggests Michael isn't smart
jokes.punchline: Michael's self-aware analysis of his narcissism
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of self-awareness from Michael about his character flaws

jokes.text: That's gonna be difficult, Rita. You're gonna be across an ocean. / Well, you can always walk. / Not on the water. / Oh, Michael, you're such a grown-up.
jokes.setup: Michael explains difficulty of visiting across ocean
jokes.punchline: Rita suggests walking on water, calls Michael grown-up for dismissing it
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to episode title setup - Rita's logic about ocean walking comes full circle

jokes.text: Hang on a sec. That's part of your trick, right? / No. / That's not my trick, Michael. / It's my illusion.
jokes.setup: Someone appears to be drowning at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Gob denies responsibility but corrects the terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob - denies causing disaster but still insists on proper terminology for magic
